mission.title: vault: Vault 2.0.0 Docker image fails to start: unable to set CAP_SETFCAP effective capability
mission.description: Vault 2.0.0 Docker image fails to start: unable to set CAP_SETFCAP effective capability. This issue affects 13+ users.

steps.title: Identify vault troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your vault installation:
```bash
vault version
vault status
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review vault configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant vault configuration:
After upgrading Docker image from `1.21.4` to `2.0.0`, the container fails during startup with the following message:

```
unable to set CAP_SETFCAP effective capability: Operation not permitted
```

This happens because the container now runs as

steps.title: Apply the fix for Vault 2.0.0 Docker image fails to start: unable to set…
steps.description:
the suggested workaround works fine for the development server, which does not use memory locking (`Mlock: supported: true, enabled: false`), but it fails in production mode.

steps.title: Confirm Vault 2.0.0 Docker image fails to start: unable… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test vault to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: the suggested workaround works fine for the development server, which does not use memory locking (`Mlock: supported: true, enabled: false`), but it fails in production mode.
